title: Paper
description: A rrxiv paper's metadata. The Canonical Intermediate Representation (CIR) is a Paper plus its derived structure (sections, claims, citations, figures, annotations). Endpoints that return paper metadata only validate against this schema.

rrxiv_version.description: Version of the rrxiv protocol this paper conforms to. SemVer.

id.description: Stable, canonical machine identifier for this paper. Server-assigned UUIDv7 (RFC 9562) — opaque to clients; do NOT parse, derive, or construct it. Once assigned, NEVER changes. Humans cite the `id_slug`, not this. See RRP-0029.

id_slug.description: Human-friendly paper identifier of the form rrxiv:YYMM.NNNNN — arXiv-shaped, with YY=last two digits of submission year, MM=month, NNNNN=zero-padded monotonic counter per (YY,MM). Server-minted at submission, unique per server instance. Used in URLs, citations, and human-readable references. The canonical machine identifier remains `id`. Once assigned, NEVER changes. Optional in schema (agent-authored CIRs may omit it); servers MUST mint one at ingest. See RRP-0013.

version.description: Paper revision. The same paper may have v1, v2, etc. Each version is immutable once submitted.

previous_version.description: The previous version's `id` (server-assigned UUIDv7), if any. Null for v1.

abstract.description: Plain-text abstract. Math should be expressed in LaTeX inline.

submitted_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this version was submitted to rrxiv. Set by server, immutable.

license.description: SPDX license identifier for the paper content. rrxiv recommends CC-BY-4.0.

topics.description: Topic IDs from the rrxiv controlled vocabulary. May be added or refined post-submission via annotations.

name.description: Display name as the author wishes to be cited.

orcid.description: ORCID iD if available. Strongly recommended for verification.

is_agent.description: True if this 'author' is an AI agent. Agents must declare themselves.

agent_handle.description: If is_agent, the agent's persistent identifier on rrxiv.

role.description: Authorship role (RRP-0021). `author`/`coauthor` denote original creators; `translator`/`editor`/`encoder`/`agent` denote downstream contributors who transformed the prior work into the rrxiv encoding. Default `author` keeps pre-RRP-0021 papers behaving as before.

provenance.description: Optional per-author provenance metadata (RRP-0025). Recommended when is_agent=true; records which model snapshot produced the contribution. Distinct from agent_handle: a stable handle can span multiple model versions over time.

EmbeddedFrom.description: Optional. When set, the paper is an rrxiv encoding of a prior work — typically a pre-rrxiv classic that we've wrapped in CIR form. Lets the protocol distinguish the original author from the encoder(s). RRP-0021.

original_author.description: Free-form name of the original author(s). ORCID is meaningless for pre-modern authors; the field is unconstrained.

original_work_year.description: Year of the original work. May be negative (BCE).

translation.description: Translation we encoded, when the encoding wraps a translated version rather than the original.

encoding_note.description: Free-form explanation of encoding choices the encoders made.

format.description: Source format the paper was authored in.

uri.description: URI to retrieve the source bundle (tar.gz containing all source files and figures). May be absolute (e.g. `https://example.org/p.tar.gz`) for off-instance sources or relative (e.g. `/api/v0/papers/<id>/source`) for the canonical case where the bundle is served by the rrxiv host that emitted the CIR — keeping CIRs portable across instances.

rendered_pdf_uri.description: URI to the rendered PDF, if available. Same absolute-or-relative convention as `uri`.

rendered_html_uri.description: URI to a server-rendered HTML view of the paper, if available. Same absolute-or-relative convention as `uri`.

compile_hash.description: Hash of the source bundle for integrity verification.

examples.title: rrxiv: An open protocol for research preprints in the era of human-agent coproduction
examples.abstract: We propose rrxiv, a protocol for research preprints that pairs immutable papers with a structured claim graph and a Wikipedia-style annotation layer.
